pi_items.verified_value: UN Human Development Report data (version unclear - data partially matches 2020 HDR, not fully aligned with 2021-22 HDR)
pi_items.source: Downloaded and analyzed official UN HDR 2021-22 Excel table and 2020 HDR Excel table. Task data shows close match with 2020 HDR for some countries (Turkey GNI=27700.9 vs CSV 27701), but mixed results overall.
Web search and direct access to UNDP website confirmed URL exists and is the official HDR 2021-22 page
Linear regression computed on task CSV data (15 data points): slope = 0.000283, p-value = 1.53e-3, which is statistically significant and positive
scipy.stats.linregress on task CSV data (15 countries, GNI vs life expectancy)
scipy.optimize.curve_fit on log_func(x) = a*ln(x)+b using task CSV data
R² comparison: 0.6868 (log) > 0.5508 (linear), consistent with README.md expectation
metadata field present but not referenced in verifier.py, README.md, or any scripts. Purpose unclear.

privileged_info_summary: The task uses real-world data from 15 countries (Norway, Switzerland, United States, Germany, United Kingdom, Japan, South Korea, Spain, Turkey, Brazil, China, Mexico, India, Nigeria, Ethiopia) representing GNI per capita (PPP USD) and life expectancy. A linear regression on this data yields a positive slope of 0.000283 years per dollar GDP (statistically significant, p=0.0015), confirming the expected positive relationship between income and life expectancy. The logarithmic model provides a substantially better fit (R²=0.687) than linear (R²=0.551), demonstrating diminishing returns at higher income levels. All 15 data points are present. The data source attribution to UN Human Development Report 2021-22 is partially inaccurate: values closely match the 2020 HDR (with 2019 data) for some countries but diverge for others, suggesting either a different source year or manually curated/rounded values.
